label: Inflation Rate
description: Headline consumer price inflation (year-on-year). NBS rebased the CPI series in January 2025, causing a step-down in the index — the underlying price pressure did not suddenly collapse.
note: ⚠︎ NBS rebased CPI in Jan 2025 — pre- and post-rebase figures are not directly comparable.

label: Naira / USD (official)
description: Official CBN exchange rate (NAFEM window). The June 2023 unification collapsed multiple rates into one market-determined rate, causing an immediate sharp devaluation from ~₦460 to ~₦770.
note: Rates shown are mid-month official NAFEM window averages.

label: Petrol Pump Price
description: Average retail pump price for Premium Motor Spirit (petrol) in Lagos. Subsidy was removed on 29 May 2023, triggering an immediate 3× price jump. Prices have continued to rise with exchange rate and crude oil movements.
note: Prices shown are Lagos ex-depot/retail averages.

label: GDP Growth Rate
description: Year-on-year real GDP growth as reported by the NBS. Growth has been positive but below the administration's 7% target, driven mainly by services and a recovering oil sector rather than broad-based economic transformation.
note: Figures are year-on-year real GDP growth rates per quarter.

label: Unemployment Rate
description: National unemployment rate per NBS Labour Force Survey. NBS changed its methodology in 2023 to align with ILO standards, which lowered the measured rate significantly from the previously reported ~33%. The figures are not comparable across the methodological break.
note: ⚠︎ NBS adopted ILO methodology in 2023 — figures not comparable with pre-2023 NBS data.
